name: Islamic Society of Central Jersey
mission: We aspire to be the anchor of a model community of practicing Muslims of diverse backgrounds, democratically governed, efficiently served, relating to one another with inclusiveness and tolerance, and interacting with the neighbors and the community at large in an Islamic exemplary fashion.‍The fundamental objective of the Society will be to seek Glory to Allah Subhanahu Wa Ta'ala. ‍The Quran, Sunnah and the Sharia shall always be the guiding light for the Society.
theoryOfChange: The Islamic Society of Central Jersey (ISCJ) undertook a year-long strategic planning journey with Oaktree Institute from October 2024 through October 2025. This process aimed to clarify ISCJ's vision, align its leadership, and create a sustainable roadmap for the future. The journey involved working sessions to finalize outcomes, Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), and a multi-year roadmap, focusing on translating vision into tangible action with clear benchmarks for tracking progress.
scoreSummary: Islamic Society of Central Jersey shows good alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

dimension_explanations.alignment: ISCJ demonstrates a medium donor fit by providing essential services that align with religious and cultural urgency, including funeral services and endowment funds [4][7]. Its track record is established through decades of community service and a transparent Zakat policy that facilitates religious giving [2][11].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on capacity building through religious and social services, reporting total revenue of $2,240,124 to support its mission [1][3]. While it maintains a program expense ratio of 32.2%, its impact is evidenced by active community committees and a long history of including women in leadership roles as Trustees and Overseers [9][11].

baseline_narrative.summary: The Islamic Society of Central Jersey (ISCJ) is a non-profit organization dedicated to serving the Muslim community through religious services, educational initiatives, and social welfare programs [3][5]. The organization maintains a strong focus on community support through its food pantry and the distribution of Zakat and Sadaqah to those in need [2][8].

The ISCJ reports a <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, meaning virtually no donor funds are lost to solicitation costs.

strengths_deep_dive: The ISCJ's primary strength lies in its institutional maturity and asset-heavy stability. With <cite id="3">total assets exceeding $16.7 million</cite> and minimal liabilities of <cite id="3">only $264,148</cite>, the organization is a permanent fixture of the regional landscape. This stability is bolstered by its <cite id="4">Endowment Fund (Waqf)</cite>, which aims to provide sustainable funding for community services. Furthermore, the organization demonstrates a <cite id="10">robust committee structure</cite> with <cite id="10">21 active committees</cite> managing various aspects of community life, from <cite id="7">funeral services</cite> to <cite id="6">business directories</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: The Islamic Society of Central Jersey (ISCJ) is a long-standing community institution <cite id="5">founded in 1973</cite> to provide social, religious, and cultural services to the Muslim community in New Jersey. Operating as a multi-faceted hub, the organization reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $2,240,124</cite> in its most recent filing, derived from a mix of <cite id="3">$1.18 million in contributions</cite> and over <cite id="3">$736,000 in program service revenue</cite>. The ISCJ manages a diverse portfolio of community-centric initiatives, including a <cite id="8">dedicated food pantry</cite>, <cite id="2">Zakat and Sadaqah distribution</cite>, and a <cite id="4">waqf endowment fund</cite>. Beyond immediate relief, the society emphasizes educational and spiritual growth through its <cite id="5">Learning Islam Together (LIT) program</cite> and a <cite id="5">weekend school</cite>. While the organization maintains <cite id="1">exceptional working capital reserves</cite> of over 100 months, its <cite id="3">program expense ratio of 32.2%</cite> indicates that a significant portion of annual spending is currently directed toward administrative and facility-related costs rather than direct service delivery. Despite this, the ISCJ remains a <cite id="9">pioneer in inclusive governance</cite>, having integrated <cite id="9">23 women as Trustees or Overseers</cite> throughout its history.
